title: Diagnose a hanging or slow request
description: Localize the stall to DNS, connect, TLS, server processing, or body transfer using timings and a packet/trace view.
body: Steps: 1. Reproduce and capture per-phase timings (DNS, TCP connect, TLS handshake, time-to-first-byte, body transfer). 2. Identify which phase dominates: slow DNS, connect refused/timeout, TLS negotiation, or server think-time. 3. Inspect with a packet capture or client trace to see retransmits, resets, or a half-open connection. 4. Check the client's timeout config against where it actually stalls. 5. Fix the phase: resolver, pool sizing, timeout, or escalate a server-side slowness. Rollback point: diagnostic only, read-only. Validation: the dominant phase is identified from timings/trace and the fix moves that number, not just masks it.
